title: Scanner is_safe_name — accept package-absolute profile paths
purpose: The Scanner's is_safe_name function rejects absolute paths, but rewrite_package_action_profile (WS-011) writes absolute profile paths for package-local profiles. This mismatch means the Scanner flags valid package workflows as unsafe when listing them. Fix is_safe_name to accept paths that resolve within a known package directory.
task: Update the is_safe_name function in the Scanner to accept absolute paths that are descendants of a workflow package's profiles/ directory. Add a test confirming package-absolute paths pass while truly unsafe paths (traversal, symlinks outside repo) still fail.

requirements.title: Extend is_safe_name to accept package profile paths
requirements.spec: is_safe_name SHALL accept an absolute path when it is a descendant of a known workflow package's profiles/ directory. The function already receives enough context to resolve this — either pass the package_dir or the workflow root. Paths containing '..' or symlinks pointing outside the repository SHALL still be rejected.
acceptance: is_safe_name returns false for paths with '..' traversal
is_safe_name returns false for paths outside the repository root

requirements.title: Add tests for package-absolute profile paths
requirements.spec: Add unit tests confirming: (1) bare profile names pass (existing behavior), (2) absolute paths within a package profiles/ dir pass (new behavior), (3) traversal paths fail, (4) paths outside the repo root fail.
acceptance: At least 4 test cases covering the scenarios above

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify any orchestrator or runner code — this is a server-side Scanner fix only.
SHALL NOT change how rewrite_package_action_profile writes paths — the Scanner adapts to it.
SHALL NOT weaken safety checks for non-package paths.
verification: The Scanner lists workflow packages with bundled profiles without warnings
